text:
Thomas Adamson

Associated Press

Published:July 27, 2024 at 12:19 PM

Updated:July 27, 2024 at 8:12 PM

Recommended Videos

Thomas Adamson

Associated Press

Copyright 2024 The Associated Press. All rights reserved

Drag queen Piche prepares to perform, at the Debilly Bridge in Paris, during the opening ceremony of the 2024 Summer Olympics, Friday, July 26, 2024. (AP Photo/Tsvangirayi Mukwazhi)

PARISвАУ In an unprecedented display of inclusivity, drag queens took center stage at theParis Olympics opening ceremony,showcasing the vibrant and influential role of the French LGBTQ+ community вАФ while also attracting criticism over a tableau reminiscent of вАЬThe Last Supper.вАЭ

Held along the Seine River, thespectacular four-hour eventfeatured global stars such asCeline DionandLady Gaga,both considered queer icons. The ceremony blendedhistoric and modern French culturewith a touch of kitsch, culminating in a flotilla of barges carrying thousands of Olympians.

Recommended Videos

Nicky Doll, known for competing on the 12th season of вАЬRuPaulвАЩs Drag RaceвАЭ and hosting вАЬDrag Race France,вАЭ participated in a high-octane fashion runway segment along with вАЬDrag Race FranceвАЭ Season 1 winner Paloma, Season 3's Piche, and Giselle Palmer. Initially, they stood alongside the runway, gazing fiercely at the strutting models. Later, they joined in, showcasing their own style.

Le Filip, the recent winner of вАЬDrag Race France,вАЭ expressed their positive вАЬsurpriseвАЭ and вАЬprideвАЭ at the ceremonyвАЩs scale and representation.

вАЬI thought it would be a five-minute drag event with queer representation. I was amazed. It started with Lady Gaga, then we had drag queens, a huge rave, and a fire in the sky,вАЭ they said. вАЬIt felt like a crowning all over again. I am proud to see my friends and queer people on the world stage.вАЭ

Among their bold performanceswas a scene that seemed to evoke Leonardo da VinciвАЩs вАЬThe Last Supper,вАЭ featuring the drag queens and other performers in a configuration reminiscent of Jesus Christ and his apostles. This segment drew significant attention вАФ and mixed reactions.

вАЬThe (French) government knows what itвАЩs doing. They want to show themselves in the best way possible. They showed no restraints in expression,вАЭ Le Filip told The Associated Press.

On the other hand, prominentfar-right politician Marion Mar√©chaldenounced the performance on social media.

вАЬTo all the Christians of the world who are watching the Paris 2024 ceremony and felt insulted by this drag queen parody of the Last Supper, know that it is not France that is speaking but a left-wing minority ready for any provocation,вАЭ she posted on the social platform X, a sentiment that was echoed by religious conservatives internationally.

вАЬ... because decapitating Habsburgs and ridiculising central Christian events are really the FIRST two things that spring to mind when you think of #OlympicGames,вАЭEduard Habsburg,Hungary's ambassador to the Vatican, posted on X, also referencing a scene depicting the beheading of Marie Antoinette.

Thomas Jolly, the artistic director of the opening ceremony,afterward drew attention away from вАЬThe Last SupperвАЭ references, saying that hadn't been his intention.

Le Filip responded to the criticism of the scene with a touch of humor and sorrow.

вАЬIt feels like the words of somebody who didnвАЩt get on the guest list. We could all be laughing together. ItвАЩs sad to me, honestly,вАЭ they said.

Inter-LGBT President James Leperlier was more circumspect, arguing that France still hassignificant strides to makein inclusivity.

вАЬWe know in the LGBTQ community in France we are far from what the ceremony showed. ThereвАЩs much progress to do in society regarding transgender people. ItвАЩs terrible that to legally change their identity they are forced to be on trial,вАЭ Leperlier said.

He also highlighted the disparity in acceptance, saying that the community is not visible in other official ceremonies and вАЬhas difficulty being heard.вАЭ

вАЬIf you saw the opening ceremony last night youвАЩd think it was like that normally, but itвАЩs not. France tried to show what it should be and not what it is,вАЭ he said.

Theopening ceremonycame as drag and the voguing nightclub scene in France has experienced a revival. The cabaret club Madame Arthur, founded in 1946 in the ashes of World War II, is one of the worldвАЩs oldest continually running LGBTQ+ theaters. It opened as Europe was only just beginning to understand the extent of the widespread murder of members of the queer community in WWII and is currently experiencing a massive renaissance.

Drag is not just a pastime; for many minority French communities who feel alienated over tensions arising from divisive politics and scars from the anti-gay marriage protests a decade ago, itвАЩs a statement of defiance. Many gay Black and Arab youths вАФ especially those from ParisвАЩ less affluent and religiously conservative suburbs вАФ and others who feel a sense of disconnect with French societyfind voguing and drag events safe placeswhere their identities can be expressed without fear of reprisal.

Despite the backlash, Le Filip believes the opening ceremony will ultimately transcend controversy.

вАЬThe message of the show is freedom, and itвАЩs a good postcard for France,вАЭ they concluded.

___

Associated Press journalist John Leicester contributed reporting.

___

For more coverage of the Paris Olympics, visithttps://apnews.com/hub/2024-paris-olympic-games.

title: Drag queens shine at Olympics opening, but 'Last Supper' tableau draws criticism
author: Thomas Adamson, Associated Press
description: In an unprecedented display of inclusivity, drag queens took center stage at the Paris Olympics opening ceremony, showcasing the vibrant and influential role of the French LGBTQ+ community.
